sections.context: Records today are anonymous beyond the git commit author. At 50-IC scale this makes contradictions, stale records, and review queues unroutable — there is no one to ping when records conflict, anchors break, or shelf life expires. R-06 is also a hard prerequisite for R-12 (contradiction detection), which has nowhere to send the resolution without an owner. Sequencing list flags this as Next now that R-04 has shipped.
sections.approach: Add `owner` (string, e.g. `@user` or `@team`) and `status` (`draft|active|deprecated`) as built-in optional record fields, then introduce a four-link owner-resolution chain evaluated at `ml record` write time: explicit `--owner` flag → CODEOWNERS lookup against the record's file_anchors/dir_anchors → per-domain `default_owner` → git author fallback. Per-domain `require_owner: true` opts a domain into hard validation (matches the existing `required_fields` enforcement pattern from mulch-3114). Layered on top, ship `ml review` as a read-only command listing records owned by `$USER` that need attention (status=draft, near-expiry, or flagged by doctor). Chosen over alternatives because it reuses the per-domain rules plumbing already shipped, keeps JSONL as the single source of truth, and degrades gracefully when neither CODEOWNERS nor config is configured (git author is always available).

alternatives.name: Single global default_owner only (no resolution chain)
alternatives.rejected_because: Too coarse at org scale — backend records and frontend records have different teams. Per-domain default_owner is the minimum viable granularity.

alternatives.name: Owner stored externally (e.g. .mulch/owners.yaml mapping IDs to handles)
alternatives.rejected_because: Breaks JSONL-as-source-of-truth and creates a second file to keep in sync. Records carry their own owner inline.

alternatives.name: Resolve owner only at read time (e.g. during prime/review)
alternatives.rejected_because: CODEOWNERS changes over time; resolving lazily means the same record reports different owners on different days. Stamp once at write time; let `ml doctor` recompute on demand if needed.

alternatives.name: Skip CODEOWNERS, use only --owner + default_owner + git author
alternatives.rejected_because: CODEOWNERS is already the canonical org ownership map for files. Bypassing it forces orgs to duplicate that mapping in mulch.config.yaml.

steps.title: Schema: add owner and status as built-in optional record fields
steps.description: Add `owner: string` and `status: 'draft' | 'active' | 'deprecated'` (default `active`) to the base record schema in src/schemas/record.ts and the generic AJV factory. Both are optional and additive — existing records without these fields remain valid. Update src/utils/expertise.ts dedup logic to ignore status when computing dedup keys (so flipping draft → active does not create a duplicate). Add tests covering round-trip read/write/prime.

steps.title: Config: add domains.<name>.require_owner and domains.<name>.default_owner
steps.description: Extend DomainConfig in src/utils/config.ts with optional `require_owner: boolean` (default false) and `default_owner: string`. Validate at config load time: require_owner only meaningful if owner is allowed by allowed_types, default_owner must look like a handle (`@<name>`). Update mulch.config.yaml init wizard examples. Tests in test/utils/config.test.ts.

steps.title: CODEOWNERS parser utility
steps.description: Add src/utils/codeowners.ts implementing GitHub's CODEOWNERS format: blank lines and `#` comments ignored, lines are `<glob> <owner1> <owner2>...`, last matching pattern wins. Honor canonical lookup paths in order: `CODEOWNERS`, `.github/CODEOWNERS`, `docs/CODEOWNERS`. Read-only utility — no caching beyond a single load per command invocation. Export `resolveOwners(filePath: string, repoRoot: string): string[]`. Tests cover globs, comments, blanks, missing files, multi-owner lines.

steps.title: Owner resolution chain at ml record write time
steps.description: In src/commands/record.ts, after schema validation and before file write: if no --owner flag provided, walk the chain — CODEOWNERS lookup against each file_anchor/dir_anchor (first match wins) → domain.default_owner → git author email. Stamp the result onto the record. If domain.require_owner is true and the chain produces no result (impossible with git author fallback unless we are not in a git repo), exit non-zero with a clear hint pointing at --owner / CODEOWNERS / default_owner. Tests in test/commands/record.test.ts.

steps.title: ml review command: list records owned by $USER needing attention
steps.description: New src/commands/review.ts (read-only, no locking needed). Default scope: records where owner matches `$USER` (resolved from git config user.email or --owner override) AND any of: status=draft, classification=tactical with age > 10 days, classification=observational with age > 25 days, or doctor reports flagged anchors/domain violations. Flags: `--owner <handle>`, `--status <draft|active|deprecated>`, `--json`. Joins the read-only command tier (prime/query/search/status/validate/learn/ready/rank). Tests cover scope, filters, and json shape.

risks: CODEOWNERS parser edge cases — glob patterns differ subtly from gitignore, last-match-wins is the opposite of most lookup tables. Mitigate with a fixture-based test suite mirroring github.com/octokit's CODEOWNERS reference cases.

Existing records have no owner. Two options: (a) leave them unowned and let ml doctor surface the gap, (b) grandfather by stamping git-blame author on first read. Pick (a) — explicit migration is safer than silent backfill, matches how dir_anchors[] and extends: were rolled out.

Owner migration when files move: file_anchors may resolve to a different CODEOWNERS owner after a refactor. Decision: do not auto-recompute on move; ml doctor adds a check that reports drift, leaving the fix to the human owner.
@team handles vs @user handles — no validation in v1; treat as opaque strings. ml doctor can later add a CODEOWNERS-membership check.
Performance: CODEOWNERS lookup runs on every ml record. Parse once per invocation, cache in-process. ml record latency budget is already tight; benchmark against a 1000-line CODEOWNERS file.
Interaction with extends: <builtin>: custom types extending built-ins must inherit the new owner/status fields automatically. Verify in test/utils/registry.test.ts.
acceptance: owner and status round-trip through ml record → JSONL → ml prime without loss; existing records without these fields remain valid (backward compatible).
ml record in a domain with default_owner: '@platform-team' and no --owner flag stamps owner='@platform-team' on the record.
ml record in a domain with require_owner: true and no resolvable owner exits non-zero with a hint that names the missing chain links.
CODEOWNERS lookup correctly resolves a file_anchor or dir_anchor to its owner; multi-owner lines return all handles; missing CODEOWNERS file is non-fatal (chain falls through).
ml review --owner @foo lists records owned by @foo with status=draft or near-expiry classifications; --json emits structured rows with id/domain/type/owner/status/reason.
All 1120 existing tests still pass; new tests cover schema, config, CODEOWNERS parser, resolution chain, and ml review.
ROADMAP.md R-06 entry updated with a Shipped: line citing the seed id and date, mirroring R-01/R-04/R-05a/e/f format.

sections.context: Stress-testing PR #21 (release v0.8.0, 58 commits, 10 features) surfaced 20 distinct findings across two adversarial agents and one migration/E2E agent. Existing 1120-test suite passes; these are gaps the unit tests don't cover. Two are critical data-integrity bugs (silent duplicate-ID writes via ml restore; A↔B supersession cycles archive both records). Several others are HIGH-severity UX failures (hook timeout reports kill but CLI hangs; raw Bun stack traces from config typos; recipe loader accepts non-default exports despite spec). The dogfood branch (now merged) exercises 4 of the 10 features in mulch's own .mulch/ — finding #14 and #15 along the way. Bundling these into a single hardening pass keeps the v0.8.x line stable without re-cutting major releases per fix.
sections.approach: Group the 20 findings into 7 logically cohesive child seeds by code path / failure surface, prioritized P0→P3. Each child seed bundles related fixes that touch overlapping code (e.g. all three recipe loader bugs live in src/commands/setup.ts and src/utils/recipe-discovery.ts) so a single PR / test run / review naturally covers them. Critical fixes (restore dup-ID, supersession cycle) ship together because both are archive-lifecycle data integrity. Polish/UX nits batch into one trailer issue. No cross-step blocking dependencies — each child can land independently as soon as a contributor picks it up.

alternatives.name: File 20 individual issues
alternatives.rejected_because: Too granular; many fixes share files and tests, and 20 P0–P3 issues with no chaining is hard to prioritize at a glance. User explicitly asked to batch.

alternatives.name: Single monolithic 'v0.8.1 hardening' bug with 20 checkboxes
alternatives.rejected_because: Loses sd's per-issue lifecycle (assignment, status, close). Hard to parallelize across contributors.

steps.title: P0: Archive-lifecycle data integrity (ml restore dup-IDs + supersession cycles)
steps.description: Fix #1: ml restore appends without checking live expertise for the same id, silently creating duplicates that doctor's content-based dedup misses (src/utils/archive.ts:116). Fix #2: A→B + B→A circular supersession demotes both records every prune pass; both archive within 3 cycles, no cycle detection. Add pre-restore live-id scan with cross-domain-ambiguity-style error; add DFS cycle skip in supersession demotion. Test cases for both. Both are CRITICAL data-loss / corruption bugs.

steps.title: P1: Hook reliability — timeout actually kills + mutable-events doc/code drift
steps.description: Fix #3: hook with timeout_ms: 500 + sleep 60 reports timeout in error message but CLI hangs the full 60s. Cause: Promise.all over Response(stdout/stderr).text() blocks on pipe EOF, which doesn't fire after SIGKILL of sh because the descendant sleep keeps the pipe open. Fix by awaiting proc.exited first, then reading captured streams with a short fallback, or by using detached spawn. Fix #19: runHooks docstring says pre-record/pre-prime/pre-prune are mutable but only pre-record + pre-prime are in MUTABLE_EVENTS — align doc to code. HIGH severity — undermines documented hook safety contract.

steps.title: P1: Provider recipe loader hardening (throw, named exports, --list shadow)
steps.description: Fix #4: recipe install() that throws (sync) or rejects (async) crashes CLI with raw Bun stack — wrap recipe invocation in src/commands/setup.ts:767 with try/catch + 'Recipe X failed: ...' framing. Fix #5: TS recipe with no default export silently accepted because mod.default ?? mod falls through to module namespace; spec says 'must default-export' — explicit check for default presence. Fix #11: --list shows both .ts and .sh entries with the same name as separate rows with no shadow indicator on the loser; show a single row with a shadow flag matching the existing builtin-shadow pattern. All three live in setup/recipe-discovery code. HIGH severity.

steps.title: P1: Config error surfacing — wrap registry init + harden doctor against minimal configs
steps.description: Fix #6: errors thrown by buildRegistryWithCustomTypes (src/registry/init.ts:32) for disabled_types/aliases/extends/custom_types misconfig bubble as raw Bun stack traces with source excerpts. Wrap at CLI top level with formatted config-error output. Fix #9: ml doctor crashes (TypeError: undefined is not an object) when YAML omits governance or classification_defaults blocks (doctor.ts:176, 491) — defensive defaults or schema-required enforcement at readConfig. Fix #17: custom_types entry without dedup_key AND without extends crashes every command at validateCustomTypeConfig instead of failing config validation cleanly — graceful error matching the rest of the validator's style. HIGH severity — every config typo currently produces frightening output.

steps.title: P2: dir_anchors normalization + project-root containment
steps.description: Fix #8: './src/utils' is not normalized to 'src/utils', so dedup misses and a record can store ['./src/utils', 'src/utils'] as separate entries. Extend normalizeDirAnchor (src/utils/dir-anchors.ts:3) to strip './' prefix in addition to handling '' and '.'. Fix #7: --dir-anchor /etc and --dir-anchor ../../escape are accepted; resolve(root, '/etc') discards the prefix and returns /etc, so existsSync passes — abs paths and traversal bypass project-root scoping. Reject absolute paths; reject paths that resolve outside the project root. Both fixes touch the same util. MEDIUM severity.

steps.title: P2: Custom types polish — name regex docs, field types, alias collisions
steps.description: Fix #14: custom_types name regex ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ rejects hyphens; neither CHANGELOG nor init-scaffold says so. Add a doc note to CHANGELOG + the optional-knobs scaffold comment + improve the registry error to name the constraint. Fix #15: all custom-type Ajv field props typed as { type: 'string' } in src/registry/custom.ts:283-289 — flake_rate: 0.05 fails AJV, must stringify. Allow per-field type declaration in custom_types config (e.g. { flake_rate: { type: 'number' } }) while keeping string the default. Fix #16: alias whose legacy_name matches another type's base field (e.g. [name]) is accepted at init; the rewrite only fires for the owning type, but a misclassified record gets silent field renames — reject at config validation. All three are custom-type registry layer. MEDIUM severity.

steps.title: P3: Polish batch — migration wording, archive parse errors, anchor grace days, rank flag parsing, --quiet semantics
steps.description: Fix #10: 'Migration is automatic for legacy configs' implies on-disk migration of domains: string[] → Record<string, DomainConfig>, but writeConfig is only called from add/delete-domain. Either migrate-on-write across all write commands or update CHANGELOG / docs to clarify the in-memory shim. Fix #13: malformed archive line crashes search --archived / restore with raw 'JSON Parse error' — no file:line pointer. Match the targeted 'Unknown record type at file:line (id=...)' error pattern. Fix #12: grace_days: 0 decays records on the same calendar day they were recorded (uses fractional ageDays > graceDays). Either round to whole days or use >= so 0 means 'don't decay anything within the same calendar day'. Fix #18: ml rank --limit '1xyz' parses as 1, --min-score '1.5xyz' parses as 1.5 — parseInt/parseFloat lenient; reject with clear error. Fix #20: --quiet suppresses the disabled_types deprecation warning, silently writing a deprecated record type — counter-intuitive, document or surface a one-line summary even under --quiet. LOW priority docs/UX cleanup batch.

risks: Critical fixes (#1, #2) need careful test coverage so we don't paper over the symptom. Cycle detection via DFS must handle long chains and self-loops without quadratic blow-up.
Hook timeout fix (#3) interacts with the spawn API surface — naive 'kill harder' approaches can race against fast-exiting hooks. Validate against the existing hooks-integration test suite.
Recipe loader changes (#4–#5) could break already-shipping recipes that rely on the lenient default-export fallback. Audit the example recipes (examples/recipes/) and any known npm package consumers.
Custom types per-field type support (#15) is the largest scope item — risks expanding into a feature creep refactor. Keep it minimal: number, boolean, string only; skip enum/regex/union for v1.
Migration wording fix (#10) is partly docs and partly behavior — pick one and ship; do not ship half-on-disk migration.
acceptance: All 7 child seeds closed; tests added/updated in each child PR.

1120-test suite still passes; new regression tests cover at minimum: ml restore over duplicate live id, A↔B supersession cycle prune pass, hook timeout with subprocess that holds stdout open, recipe with no default export rejected, recipe install() throw produces formatted error, config missing governance block produces formatted doctor output (not crash), dir_anchor normalization for './prefix' and rejection of absolute / traversal paths, custom_types hyphenated name produces named-constraint error, alias collision with foreign-type base field rejected at init.
CHANGELOG.md updated with a [0.8.1] section listing each fix with seed id + one-line summary.
ml doctor on the dogfooded .mulch/ config (now merged) continues to pass after each fix lands.

sections.context: Warren V2 (warren ROADMAP R-10) needs every os-eco tool to publish its config JSON Schema so it can render configuration UIs generically — no per-knob form code per tool. Mulch is the highest-leverage starting point: it has the largest config surface in the ecosystem (~20 knobs across governance, classification_defaults, domains, custom_types, hooks, hook_settings, decay, search, prime, disabled_types, version) and its config is already cleanly TS-typed in src/schemas/config.ts. Shipping `ml config schema` plus atomic per-knob writes (`ml config set` / `unset` / `show`) gives warren a stable contract to render forms against and a safe write path that goes through mulch's existing file-lock + atomic-rename machinery.
sections.approach: Hand-author a JSON Schema for MulchConfig in src/schemas/config-schema.ts (mirroring the existing src/schemas/record-schema.ts convention — exported const, AJV-strict-compatible). Hand-authoring (vs. generating from TS) keeps full control over title/description/default/enum/min/max metadata that warren's UI depends on, and avoids adding a build step to a Bun-no-build-step project. Lock the shape with a golden test: intentional schema changes update the golden, accidental drift fails CI. Add a single `ml config` command in src/commands/config.ts (registered in src/cli.ts following the registerXCommand convention) with four subcommands: `schema`, `show`, `set`, `unset`. Reuse the existing concurrency primitives — withFileLock from src/utils/lock.ts and atomic-rename from the writeExpertiseFile pattern — for every write. YAML-parse user-supplied values (so warren can submit lists/objects/primitives uniformly) and validate the full resulting config against the schema before write so invalid values are rejected with a clear, schema-referencing error.

alternatives.name: Generate JSON Schema from TS interface at build time (ts-json-schema-generator)
alternatives.rejected_because: Adds a build step to a project that runs Bun directly with no dist output. Loses control over the title/description/enum/range metadata warren's UI depends on (TS type comments don't map cleanly to JSON Schema). Hand-authoring + golden test gives equivalent drift protection with less moving machinery.

alternatives.name: Single flat `ml config <op>` command with op as positional
alternatives.rejected_because: Commander's subcommand idiom (matches `sd plan <subcmd>`, `ml setup <provider>`) gives per-subcommand --help, scoped flags, and clearer testability. No upside to the flat shape.

alternatives.name: Append-style writes for list-valued knobs (`ml config set domains.warren.allowed_types '+convention'`)
alternatives.rejected_because: Out of scope for V1 per seed's open question. Replacement-only keeps the YAML-parse path uniform across all knobs; append/remove can land as a follow-up once warren's UX needs it.

steps.title: Author MulchConfig JSON Schema in src/schemas/config-schema.ts
steps.description: Create src/schemas/config-schema.ts exporting MULCH_CONFIG_SCHEMA covering every knob in MulchConfig (src/schemas/config.ts:101-137): version, domains (open map → DomainConfig), governance (max_entries/warn_entries/hard_limit), classification_defaults.shelf_life.{tactical,observational}, prime.default_mode (enum: manifest|full), search.boost_factor, custom_types (open map → CustomTypeConfig with extends/required/optional/dedup_key/id_key/summary/extracts_files/files_field/compact (enum: concat|merge_outcomes|keep_latest|manual)/section_title/aliases), decay.anchor_validity.{threshold (0..1)|grace_days (>=0)|weight}, disabled_types, hooks (HOOK_EVENTS keys → string[]), hook_settings.timeout_ms. Every property must have title + description; defaults from DEFAULT_CONFIG and the DEFAULT_* constants; enums for known unions; minimum/maximum on numeric ranges; additionalProperties:false on closed objects, additionalProperties:<schema> on the open maps (domains, custom_types, hooks). Follow mx-b0403f (always include type:'object' alongside required/properties for AJV strict mode) and mx-5baadf (schema lives in .ts, not .json).

steps.title: Add `ml config` command skeleton with `schema` subcommand
steps.description: Create src/commands/config.ts exporting registerConfigCommand(program) per the mx-60f9f9 add-command guide. Wire a `config` parent command with a `schema` subcommand: `ml config schema [--json]`. JSON mode emits the JSON Schema document straight from MULCH_CONFIG_SCHEMA via JSON.stringify(_, null, 2); non-JSON mode prints a human-readable summary (top-level knobs grouped by section, one line each). Register the command in src/cli.ts alongside the other 26. No write path yet — pure read.

steps.title: Add golden test for `ml config schema --json`
steps.description: Add test/commands/config.test.ts following the no-mocks convention (mx-53d431) — real filesystem temp dirs via mkdtemp, assertions against actual stdout. Snapshot the full `ml config schema --json` output to test/fixtures/config-schema.golden.json. Test asserts deep equality. Document in the test file how to update: re-run with UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 (or equivalent) when the schema legitimately changes.

steps.title:
Add `ml config show [--path

] [--json]` subcommand
steps.description: Add a `show` subcommand to ml config. With no --path, emits the resolved config (DEFAULT_CONFIG-backfilled per mx-3b7876, so a minimal user config still produces full output). With --path <dot-notation>, traverses (e.g. `governance.max_entries`, `domains.warren.allowed_types`, `custom_types.release_decision.required`) and emits only that subtree. --json forces JSON output (default), non-JSON pretty-prints YAML. Read-only — no locking needed. Reuse readConfig from src/utils/config.ts.

steps.title: Add `ml config set <path> <value>` subcommand with atomic write
steps.description: Add a `set` subcommand. Parse <value> as YAML (yaml.load) so warren can submit `'[a, b]'` for lists, `'{key: val}'` for objects, or bare scalars uniformly. Walk the dot-notation path (creating intermediate objects for open maps like domains.<name>, custom_types.<name>, hooks.<event>), set the leaf, then run the resulting full config through Ajv against MULCH_CONFIG_SCHEMA. Reject invalid values with an error that quotes the schema's title/description for the offending field. On success: write through withFileLock(configPath, ...) (src/utils/lock.ts) using a temp-file + rename atomic write that mirrors writeExpertiseFile. Auto-create .mulch/mulch.config.yaml if absent (matches `ml init` UX, per seed's lean-yes). Print a canonical YAML diff (or 'set <path>: <value>' confirmation) on success. Reuse getMulchDir from src/utils/git.ts so worktrees route to the main repo's config (mx-376671 / worktree-aware storage).

steps.title: Add `ml config unset <path>` subcommand
steps.description:
Add an `unset` subcommand. Walks the dot-notation path and deletes the leaf (or the whole nested object if path points to one). Re-runs Ajv validation against the post-delete config — if the delete leaves something required missing, emit a clear error and abort. Same withFileLock + atomic-rename write path as `set`. After delete, the value reverts to whatever DEFAULT_CONFIG / DEFAULT_* constant supplies (verified by `ml config show --path

` returning the default). Refuses to unset top-level required fields (version, domains, governance, classification_defaults).

steps.title: Worktree + concurrency integration tests across all subcommands
steps.description: Add integration tests in test/commands/config.test.ts that: (a) run every subcommand from inside a `git worktree add` checkout and assert writes land in the main repo's .mulch/mulch.config.yaml (covers worktree-aware getMulchDir per mx-376671); (b) launch two concurrent `ml config set` processes against different paths and assert both land without corrupting the YAML (covers withFileLock); (c) run `ml config set` with invalid value (e.g. governance.max_entries: 'not a number') and assert non-zero exit + schema-referencing error; (d) run `ml config set` against an absent .mulch/mulch.config.yaml and assert it auto-creates with the new knob plus DEFAULT_CONFIG backfill. Confirm `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` all green.

risks: Hand-authored JSON Schema drifts from the TS MulchConfig interface as new knobs land — golden test catches output drift but not the upstream-TS-vs-schema drift. Mitigation: doctor check or test that walks Object.keys of DEFAULT_CONFIG and asserts each appears in the schema (and vice versa for required top-level fields).

YAML parsing of <value> has surprising edges: bare `yes`/`no` parse as booleans, `1.0` as number, empty string as null. Warren may submit JSON-stringified values that re-parse unexpectedly. Mitigation: test the common cases (boolean knob, numeric knob, list, object, string-with-special-chars) and document the YAML-input contract in the subcommand --help.

Dot-notation traversal into open maps (domains.<name>, custom_types.<name>) needs to create intermediate objects when absent, but must NOT silently create them for closed-shape paths (e.g. `ml config set governance.typo 5` should error, not insert a new field). Mitigation: walk the schema in parallel with the path; reject when the next segment isn't a known property AND the current node is closed (additionalProperties:false).

Auto-creating .mulch/mulch.config.yaml on `ml config set` may surprise users running from outside a mulch project. Mitigation: only auto-create when getMulchDir() resolves successfully (i.e., we're in a git repo with a .mulch/ in the main worktree); otherwise hard-error pointing to `ml init`.

Schema versioning across mulch releases: warren caches the schema per mulch version. If MulchConfig.version bumps from '1' to '2' with breaking changes, warren needs the new shape. Mitigation (V1): emit the current-version schema unconditionally. Document that warren should key its cache on `mulch --version --json` output.

Concurrent `ml config set` writes against the same key could race. The advisory file lock serializes them, but last-writer-wins is the only safe semantic — no conflict detection. Mitigation: doc this in --help; warren UI should re-fetch via `ml config show` after every write.
acceptance: `ml config schema --json` emits a complete JSON Schema document covering every knob in MulchConfig, with title, description, default (where applicable), enum (for unions), and minimum/maximum (for numeric ranges).
Golden test in test/commands/config.test.ts asserts the full schema shape; accidental drift fails CI, intentional changes update the golden via documented procedure.

`ml config set <path> <yaml-value>` parses the value as YAML, validates the resulting full config against the schema, and atomically writes via withFileLock; invalid values are rejected with an error that references the schema's title/description for the offending field.

`ml config show [--path

] [--json]` reads any knob (or the whole config) and emits stable JSON output suitable for warren consumption.

`ml config unset ` removes the knob from disk and `ml config show --path

` subsequently returns the schema default.
All four subcommands work correctly from inside a `git worktree add` checkout (writes land in the main repo's .mulch/mulch.config.yaml).
`bun test`, `bun run lint`, and `bun run typecheck` all pass with no new warnings.

sections.context: Warren V2 (ROADMAP R-11) wants to inject mulch-primed expertise directly into the system prompt sent to coding agents at run-spawn time. The flow shells `ml prime --domain X --files F --budget N --format plain` and concatenates the output into the role's system prompt. For a UI preview, warren also wants `--dry-run` that returns metadata (record IDs, types, domains, token counts, budget usage) without rendering content. `--format plain` already exists in mulch (global flag at src/cli.ts:120; formatDomainExpertisePlain / formatPrimeOutputPlain / formatPrimeManifestPlain in src/utils/format.ts), but no audit has been done against the spawn-injection contract, and prime.ts unconditionally appends the Session Close Protocol reminder (src/commands/prime.ts:365 via getSessionEndReminder) which would pollute a system prompt. `--dry-run` does not exist on prime today.
sections.approach: Two narrow, additive changes to src/commands/prime.ts and src/utils/format.ts, plus an audit pass on the existing plain formatters. (1) Audit the existing `formatDomainExpertisePlain` / `formatPrimeOutputPlain` output against the spawn-injection contract from the seed (no markdown headers, no code fences, no decorative ASCII, no leading 'primed at <timestamp>' header) and tighten only what diverges — minimize blast radius on existing search/query --format plain consumers. Suppress the trailing Session Close Protocol reminder when format=plain, since warren handles session framing contextually. (2) Add a `--dry-run` flag that short-circuits content rendering and emits JSON: `{ wouldPrime: [{id, type, domain, tokens}], totalTokens, budgetUsed, budgetTotal }`. Reuse the existing budget/token estimation pipeline (applyBudget + estimateRecordText in src/utils/budget.ts) so dry-run numbers match what real prime would emit, and respect the budget (only list what fits) per the seed's lean-in answer. `--format plain --dry-run` returns the JSON summary (format ignored when dry-running). Both flags compose with existing --domain, --files, --budget.

alternatives.name: New top-level `ml plan` or `ml preview` command
alternatives.rejected_because: prime is the right entry point; format and dry-run are presentation concerns, not new capabilities. Adding a parallel command duplicates filtering/budget logic.

alternatives.name: Make `plain` the default format on non-TTY stdout
alternatives.rejected_because: Per the seed's open-question lean: keep the current default. Explicit `--format plain` is clearer and won't surprise existing pipe consumers (CI scripts, agent runners that capture output).

alternatives.name: Include a content fragment (first ~50 chars per record) in dry-run output
alternatives.rejected_because: Per the seed's lean: keep dry-run lightweight. Warren can fetch full content via `ml show <id>` if richer preview is ever needed; bloating dry-run defeats its purpose as a fast UI feedback path.

alternatives.name: Add a `--summary` flag instead of `--dry-run`
alternatives.rejected_because: `--dry-run` is the conventional name and matches `ml compact --dry-run` / `ml prune --dry-run` precedent in this codebase. Consistency wins.

steps.title: Audit existing `--format plain` output against the spawn-injection contract
steps.description: Run `ml prime --format plain` against the current repo's .mulch/ and capture the output. Verify against the seed's contract: (a) no markdown headers/code fences/decorative ASCII, (b) one record per stanza separated by blank lines, (c) no 'primed at <timestamp>' header, (d) no trailing Session Close Protocol reminder (currently appended unconditionally at src/commands/prime.ts:365). Also exercise `--manifest --format plain` (formatPrimeManifestPlain) for completeness. Document any divergence in a short note attached to this plan. Output of this step is a punch list for step 2 — no code changes here.

steps.title: Tighten `--format plain` rendering to match the spawn-injection contract
steps.description: Address the punch list from step 1. At minimum: skip the `getSessionEndReminder(format)` append in src/commands/prime.ts when format === 'plain' (the same `output += \n\n${getSessionEndReminder(format)}` block runs at lines ~227 for manifest and ~365 for full prime — handle both call sites). If the audit also flags formatDomainExpertisePlain or formatPrimeOutputPlain noise, fix it in src/utils/format.ts. Be conservative: search.ts and query.ts also call these formatters via the global --format flag (src/commands/search.ts:274, src/commands/query.ts:187), so any change must keep their output sensible. Update test/commands/prime.test.ts assertions on plain output if shape changes.

steps.title: Implement `--dry-run` JSON output on `ml prime`
steps.description: Add `.option('--dry-run', 'emit metadata about what would be primed without rendering content')` to the prime command in src/commands/prime.ts. When set, run the full record-loading + filtering + budget pipeline (so wouldPrime reflects what real prime would emit), but emit `{ wouldPrime: [{id, type, domain, tokens}], totalTokens, budgetUsed, budgetTotal }` as JSON to stdout instead of formatted records. Reuse `estimateRecordText` from src/utils/budget.ts for per-record token counts so numbers match real budget enforcement. `budgetTotal` reflects --budget (or DEFAULT_BUDGET); `--no-limit` should set budgetTotal to null and budgetUsed to null. Skip `getSessionEndReminder` and the pre-prime hook output mutation path (dry-run is a preview, not a write — same pattern as `ml prune --dry-run` per the soft-archive-prune precedent). When --dry-run is combined with --format, ignore --format and emit JSON regardless. When combined with --export, write JSON to the file.

steps.title: Add tests covering plain format + dry-run + composition
steps.description: Extend test/commands/prime.test.ts with three test groups: (a) `--format plain` produces clean text — assert no markdown headers ('# '), no code fences ('```'), no Session Close Protocol marker (e.g. '🚨' or 'Session Close Protocol' substring), records appear as expected stanzas; (b) `--dry-run` returns valid JSON with wouldPrime array (each entry has id/type/domain/tokens), totalTokens equal to sum of tokens, budgetUsed equal to totalTokens/budgetTotal, --no-limit produces null budget fields; (c) composition: `--format plain --dry-run` returns JSON (format ignored), `--dry-run --domain <X>` filters to domain X, `--dry-run --files <path>` filters by file relevance, `--dry-run --budget 1000` truncates wouldPrime to fit. Follow the no-mocks convention (mx-53d431) — write real .mulch/expertise/<domain>.jsonl fixtures via mkdtemp and assert on real CLI output.

steps.title: Update docs: CHANGELOG, README, command help text
steps.description: Add an entry under [Unreleased] in CHANGELOG.md (Keep a Changelog format per mx-900a53) describing the new `--dry-run` flag and the plain-format polish for spawn-time injection. Add a short README section under prime usage showing the warren spawn-injection example: `ml prime --domain X --files F --budget N --format plain` and the dry-run preview shape. Update the option help string for `--format plain` in src/cli.ts if the current wording doesn't mention spawn injection. Per mx-6ac234, keep README in sync.

risks: Tightening `formatDomainExpertisePlain` or `formatPrimeOutputPlain` could regress existing `ml search --format plain` and `ml query --format plain` consumers (src/commands/search.ts:274, src/commands/query.ts:187). Mitigation: keep changes additive (suppress trailers in prime.ts call sites rather than mutating shared formatters) wherever possible.

`estimateRecordText` in budget.ts historically only switched over the 6 built-in record types; custom-typed records were unestimated (mx-8662bd). Dry-run must dispatch via the type registry to avoid undercounting tokens for custom types. Verify this is already routed through the registry before relying on it.

Suppressing the Session Close Protocol reminder per format risks fragmenting the cross-tool 'session close' pattern (mx-4d72e9). Document the intent: warren handles session framing in its own dispatch, so the reminder would be redundant and noisy in a system prompt.

Dry-run that respects the budget could surprise users who expect 'show everything that matched my filter'. The seed answers this (lean: respect budget) — surface it in the help text so the contract is explicit.
If --dry-run runs the pre-prime hook, hook side effects (e.g. Slack posts) would fire on every preview. Per soft-archive-prune precedent (mx-a7b234), dry-run should skip hooks entirely.
acceptance: `ml prime --format plain` emits clean text suitable for system-prompt concatenation: no markdown headers, no code fences, no decorative ASCII, no leading 'primed at <timestamp>' header, no trailing Session Close Protocol reminder.
`ml prime --dry-run` emits JSON `{ wouldPrime: [{id, type, domain, tokens}], totalTokens, budgetUsed, budgetTotal }` without rendering record content; numbers match what real prime would emit.
`ml prime --format plain --dry-run` is a valid combination and returns the dry-run JSON summary (format ignored when dry-running).
Both flags compose with existing `--domain`, `--files`, `--budget`, `--no-limit`, `--exclude-domain` flags.
Test coverage in test/commands/prime.test.ts asserts plain shape + dry-run JSON shape + composition; `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` pass.
CHANGELOG.md and README.md updated per project conventions (mx-900a53, mx-6ac234).

outcomeNote: All 5 child steps implemented: plain audit + tightened formatPrimeOutputPlain (preserved formatDomainExpertisePlain for search/query), --dry-run with budget/hook precedence, latent --no-limit bug fixed (Commander parses as options.limit), 9 new tests, README + CHANGELOG updated. 1181 tests pass.
